**Acceptance Criteria**
CFGA-01 and CFGA-02 appear functionally implemented: `validate_scope` now derives `breakdown_type` through the container’s template at [validate.rs:673](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/validate.rs:673), and `resolve_plan_content` derives breakable types/doc locations from `TemplateRegistry` at [validate.rs:462](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/validate.rs:462). Tests cover custom template boundary and external plan docs in [scope_validation_tests.rs:276](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/tests/scope_validation_tests.rs:276) and [scope_validation_tests.rs:463](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/tests/scope_validation_tests.rs:463).

**Blocking Finding**
Documentation debt was introduced/left unresolved in public API docs. [queries.rs:173](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/domain/queries.rs:173) still says the boundary is “config-driven” and comes from `[planning].breakdown_type`; [queries.rs:179](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/domain/queries.rs:179) says `None` means no `[planning]` block. That directly contradicts this issue’s new template-registry source of truth.

The plan-doc module docs also still describe `[planning].plan_doc_location` and `PlanningConfig` as the selector at [plan_doc.rs:3](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/plan_doc.rs:3), and `INLINE_LOCATION` is still documented as a `[planning]` convention at [plan_doc.rs:32](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/plan_doc.rs:32). Public docs must match behavior; this is technical debt and fails the review standard.

**Architecture And Separation**
No new boundary violation found. Filesystem reads remain in command/plan-doc boundary code, and the domain graph walk stays pure.

**Functional Paradigm**
The new code mostly uses iterator combinators and expression-oriented flow. No blocking imperative churn found.

**Correctness And Invariants**
No new file writes, event-log mutations, gate transitions, PID handling, assignee parsing, or label parsing paths were introduced in the Rust changes. Existing `.jit` state changes are accompanied by event entries. No blocking correctness issue found beyond the stale public docs.

**Safety**
No unsafe code observed in the changed paths. The recorded `cargo-ci` gate includes clippy success.

**Testing**
Used the provided passed `cargo-ci` gate as primary evidence: `fmt ok`, `clippy ok`, `2142 passed, 0 failed, 9 ignored` in `.jit/gate-runs/78b9a0d1-f48f-422e-8a77-ba56049078e6/result.json`. I did not rerun cargo tests. I did run `./scripts/jit-validate.sh`; it passed with label warnings only, which are allowed.

**Error Handling**
The new fallible plan-content loading still returns `Result` and adds contextual errors through existing `load_plan_content`. No new `unwrap()`/`expect()` in library code found in the reviewed changes.

**CLI Contract**
No CLI command surface was changed, so no new `--json` contract issue found.

**Prior Review Feedback**
`run_history` is empty, so there were no prior review findings to verify.

**Jit Issue Management**
The listed dependency `3143ad87` is marked done. `./scripts/jit-validate.sh` passed with warnings only.

VERDICT: FAIL
